2018-01-26.abstract: United States cooperation with the Kurdish group has complicated ties with Turkey, which views them as terrorists.

2018-01-26.title: South Korea Hospital Fire Kills at Least 37 People
2018-01-26.abstract: The deadly blaze was the latest in a string of a tragedies that have heightened public concern over the country’s safety standards.

2018-01-26.title: China Embraces a Game About a Traveling Frog
2018-01-26.abstract: For whatever reason, Travel Frog, an extremely low-key Japanese mobile game, is a hit in China. “You don’t even need to think about anything,” one player explained.

2018-01-26.title: Deadly Fire Undermines South Korean Leader’s Vow to Make Country Safer
2018-01-26.abstract: The fire that killed at least 37 people comes as President Moon Jae-in’s approval ratings are falling over his decision to form a unified Olympic team with North Korea.

2018-01-26.title: Floods Leave Paris Contemplating a Wetter Future
2018-01-26.abstract: The Seine River overflowed its banks for the second time in less than two years, raising questions about a potential pattern.

2018-01-27.title: Saudi Arabia Frees Media Mogul, but His Company’s Fate Remains a Mystery
2018-01-27.abstract: MBC’s owner resisted a purchase offer from Saudi Arabia’s crown prince. He was among those seized in what the Saudis called a corruption crackdown.

2018-01-27.title: Deaths of Billionaire Toronto Couple Were Homicides, Police Say
2018-01-27.abstract: Toronto authorities have ruled out murder-suicide, but they have no suspects yet in the mysterious killings.

2018-01-27.title: Free Trade Disillusionment and the First Hipsters: The Canada Letter
2018-01-27.abstract: This week’s Canada letter looks at the decline in manufacturing and some remarkably clear images from the 19th century gold rushes.

2018-01-27.title: Hong Kong Bars Democracy Advocate From Running for Legislature
2018-01-27.abstract: Agnes Chow, 21, whose party says Hong Kong should decide its future in a referendum, said the ruling showed “that our political rights are handicapped.”

2018-01-27.title: ‘It’s a Massacre’: Blast in Kabul Deepens Toll of a Long War
2018-01-27.abstract: At least 95 were killed and 158 others were wounded when a car bomber drove an ambulance past two checkpoints and detonated explosives.

2018-01-28.title: Pakistan Army ‘Has Greatly Increased Its Clout’ Under New Chief
2018-01-28.abstract: Since assuming what may be the most powerful position in Pakistan, Gen. Qamar Javed Bajwa has left no doubt about who is in charge, even as he deals with a freeze in aid from the Trump administration.

2018-01-28.title: Bolivia Tells President His Time Is Up. He Isn’t Listening.
2018-01-28.abstract: Voters told President Evo Morales in a referendum it was time to retire. But he intends to run again, part of a tilt toward authoritarian tendencies among both the left and right in Latin America.

2018-01-28.title: Russians Brave Icy Temperatures to Protest Putin and Election
2018-01-28.abstract: Demonstrators turned out across the country against what they called a lack of choice in the March 2018 presidential vote, which is almost certain to give President Vladimir Putin a fourth term.

2018-01-28.title: Climber Rescued on Pakistan’s ‘Killer Mountain,’ but Another Is in Peril
2018-01-28.abstract: An elite team came to the rescue of the French climber Elisabeth Revol, but extremely harsh conditions made it impossible to reach her Polish partner, Tomek Mackiewicz.

2018-01-28.title: Iranians Prayed for Rain, but Were Covered in Snow
2018-01-28.abstract: The country has been gripped by a terrible drought, so a blizzard brought joy — although the relief was most likely only temporary.

2018-01-29.title: Olympic Dreams of a United Korea? Many in South Say, ‘No, Thanks’
2018-01-29.abstract: Young Koreans are more focused on domestic issues, like unemployment and whether they can live as well as their parents did, than a costly and complex reunification.

2018-01-29.title: Why Attack Afghan Civilians? Creating Chaos Rewards Taliban
2018-01-29.abstract: The central issue in the 16-year war has become whether Afghanistan can have a functioning state. The Taliban have every incentive to expose government weakness.

2018-01-29.title: Attack Near Kabul Military Academy Kills 11 Afghan Soldiers
2018-01-29.abstract: Suicide bombers targeted an army unit beside the main Afghan military university, adding to the toll of a violent stretch in the Afghan capital.

2018-01-29.title: Kiribati Ferry Search Expands After 7 Are Found Alive
2018-01-29.abstract: The survivors were found “in the baking sun” after more than a week without supplies, shade or a motor for their dinghy.

2018-01-29.title: Fire Breaks Out at Taiwan Refinery
2018-01-29.abstract: The blaze in Taoyuan, home to the international airport, went on for hours, and the city fined the company that owns the facility for contributing to air pollution.

2018-01-30.title: With Tour and White House Lunch, Trump Presses U.N. Envoys on Iran
2018-01-30.abstract: The administration said it had shown Security Council diplomats proof that Iran was arming Yemen rebels. Iran dismissed it as “Iranphobic” fakery.

2018-01-30.title: In Colombia, Two Rebel Groups Take Different Paths
2018-01-30.abstract: The FARC, whose members once terrorized the country, was holding a campaign rally this weekend. The E.L.N., a rebel group, was setting off bombs.

2018-01-30.title: Berlusconi Is Back. Again. This Time, as Italy’s ‘Nonno’
2018-01-30.abstract: A master of reinvention, the three-time former prime minister is poised to come out a winner in Italy’s election. Just spare him the Trump comparisons.

2018-01-30.title: Taiwan Retaliates Against Chinese Airlines, Hampering Lunar New Year Travel
2018-01-30.abstract: Taiwan, pushing back over encroachment on Taiwan Strait airspace, may leave thousands without flights home for the holiday.

2018-01-30.title: How Australia’s Espionage Laws Could Silence Whistle-Blowers and Activists
2018-01-30.abstract: Australia wants to lead the world in combating foreign interference in politics. But critics say the laws could make the country more like the regimes it aims to resist.

2018-01-31.title: Saudi-Backed Coalition in Yemen Upended in Deadly Factional Fighting
2018-01-31.abstract: Three days of combat between factions of the Saudi-backed coalition in the southern Yemeni city of Aden has further complicated the war.

2018-01-31.title: Syrian Peace Talks in Russia: 1,500 Delegates, Mostly Pro-Assad
2018-01-31.abstract: With United Nations-sponsored talks to end the civil war getting nowhere, Russia staged its own. Neither has made much progress.

2018-01-31.title: Trump Vows a Muscular America to Confront a World of Enemies
2018-01-31.abstract: Celebrating gains against the Islamic State but warning of threats like Iran and North Korea, President Trump sketched a dark view of a perilous world.

2018-01-31.title: North Korean Defector, Honored by Trump, Has a Remarkable Escape Story
2018-01-31.abstract: Ji Seong-ho, a defector from North Korea who lost an arm and a leg at 13, traveled thousands of miles on crutches to make it to the South.

2018-01-31.title: Taiwan Citizens Accused of Violating U.N. Sanctions on North Korea
2018-01-31.abstract: The case comes after another Taiwanese citizen has faced accusations of conducting trade in violation of United Nations sanctions against North Korea.
